thread.name:
Work on this ticket

Overview

Generalize the existing task-specific comment infrastructure in lib/core/communications/channels/comments/ to support any entity type. This is the foundational work that enables all subsequent migrations and new comment capabilities.

What Changes

1. Schema: Extend ConversationReferenceType enum

File: apps/dashboard/database/prisma/schema/communication.prisma

Add ELIGIBILITY_CHECK, CONTACT, PAYER to the ConversationReferenceType enum

Add manualVerificationNotes String? inline field to EligibilityCheck model

Run pnpm --filter database migrate dev && pnpm --filter database generate

2. Generalize comment helpers

Directory: apps/dashboard/lib/core/communications/channels/comments/

Refactor task-specific helpers to accept referenceType + referenceId:

Current (Task-Specific)

Generalized

buildTaskConversationReference(taskId)

buildConversationReference(referenceType, referenceId)

extractTaskReference(conversation)

extractEntityReference(conversation) returning { referenceType, referenceId }

fetchCommentResources(orgId, taskId, ...)

fetchCommentResources(orgId, referenceType, referenceId, ...)

validateCommentAuthorization(orgId, authorId, ...)

validateCommentAuthorization(orgId, referenceType, referenceId, authorId, ...)

createCommentCommunication({ taskId, ... })

createCommentCommunication({ referenceType, referenceId, ... })

listCommentCommunications({ taskId, ... })

listCommentCommunications({ referenceType, referenceId, ... })

3. Entity validation registry

File: apps/dashboard/lib/core/communications/channels/comments/entity-validators.ts

Create a Record<ConversationReferenceType, EntityValidator> mapping

Each validator checks the referenced entity exists and user has access

Start with TASK, PATIENT, ELIGIBILITY_CHECK; add CONTACT and PAYER stubs for Phases 3-4

4. Update inbox filter

File: list-conversations.ts in communications bounded context

Replace { referenceType: { not: ConversationReferenceType.TASK } } with { referenceType: { not: { in: ENTITY_COMMENT_REFERENCE_TYPES } } }

Define ENTITY_COMMENT_REFERENCE_TYPES constant

Acceptance Criteria

ConversationReferenceType enum includes ELIGIBILITY_CHECK, CONTACT, PAYER

EligibilityCheck model has manualVerificationNotes field

Comment helpers accept any ConversationReferenceType (not just TASK)

Entity validator registry is in place with validators for TASK, PATIENT, ELIGIBILITY_CHECK

Inbox filter excludes all entity-comment reference types

Existing task comments continue to work (no regression)

All checks pass: pnpm lint:fix && pnpm format:fix && pnpm typecheck:native && pnpm lint:custom

References

ADR: Unified Notes System (Phase 1 section)

Current task comment implementation: lib/core/communications/channels/comments/

Communication schema: apps/dashboard/database/prisma/schema/communication.prisma

userMessages.content:
Delivery Loop phase advanced to implementing. Execute the approved plan now.

Implement the planned changes directly in code.

After implementing, signal phaseComplete: true again to trigger mandatory review, UI smoke, and PR steps.

Delivery Loop review phase blocked. Fix all blocking Deep/Carmack findings, then signal phaseComplete: true again.

Deep review blocking findings:
1. [HIGH] Task delete route can delete non-task comments before rejecting (authorization)
In `apps/dashboard/trpc/routers/web/tasks/tasks/procedures/delete-task-comment.ts`, task association is checked only after `deleteCommentCommunication()` runs. Since `fetchCommentForDeletion()` no longer constrains conversation reference type to TASK, a caller can delete a PATIENT/ELIGIBILITY_CHECK/etc comment by ID, then receive `BAD_REQUEST` after the deletion already committed.
2. [HIGH] Task update/star routes can mutate non-task comments before rejecting (authorization)
In `update-task-comment.ts` and `toggle-star-comment.ts`, task association is derived in `buildCommentDto` after `updateCommentCommunication()` / `toggleStarComment()` have already written changes. Core helpers now accept any entity reference and no longer enforce TASK-only behavior, so task-scoped endpoints can update/star comments for other entities and then throw `BAD_REQUEST` after the write.

Carmack review blocking findings:
1. [HIGH] Entity validators do not enforce per-user access (authorization)
The new validator contract in `entity-validators.ts` only accepts `(organizationId, referenceId)` and `validateCommentAuthorization` only verifies org membership before calling it. This cannot satisfy the requirement that validators ensure the user has access to the referenced entity; it only checks entity existence in-org. For non-task entities, this can allow comment creation on records a user should not access.
2. [HIGH] Recipient discovery is no longer scoped to comment-thread conversations (data-isolation)
In `helpers/fetch-resources.ts`, `existingParticipantIds` now queries by `(referenceType, referenceId)` only, after removing conversation-type scoping. If any non-comment conversation shares that entity reference, its participants are pulled into comment recipient construction, causing unintended participant propagation and potential PHI exposure.

Delivery Loop review phase blocked. Fix all blocking Deep/Carmack findings, then signal phaseComplete: true again.

Deep review blocking findings:
1. [HIGH] Comment listing query no longer scopes to comment threads (data-leak/regression)
In `apps/dashboard/lib/core/communications/channels/comments/list-comments.ts`, the `findMany` filter now matches only `conversation.referenceType` + `referenceId`. The previous task-scoped guard (`conversation.type === TASK`) was removed, and there is still no `communication.kind === COMMENT` guard. This can pull in non-comment communications from other conversation types that reuse the same reference pair, causing timeline pollution and potential cross-channel data exposure.

Carmack review blocking findings:
1. [HIGH] Comment listing query no longer constrains conversation channel type (architectural-correctness)
In apps/dashboard/lib/core/communications/channels/comments/list-comments.ts:36-43, the query now filters only by {referenceType, referenceId} and dropped the prior ConversationType guard. This allows any future/current non-comment conversation that reuses the same entity reference to be returned as a comment, which breaks channel isolation and can surface incorrect timeline data.

Delivery Loop review phase blocked. Fix all blocking Deep/Carmack findings, then signal phaseComplete: true again.

Carmack review blocking findings:
1. [HIGH] Deletion/reference lookup now hard-requires `ConversationType.TASK`, breaking legacy entity-comment conversations (architectural-correctness)
`fetchCommentForDeletion` was changed from filtering by `conversation.referenceType === TASK` to `conversation.type === TASK` (apps/dashboard/lib/core/communications/channels/comments/helpers/fetch-for-deletion.ts). The new task TRPC flows (`delete-task-comment`, `toggle-star-comment`, `update-task-comment`) now depend on `getCommentEntityReference`, which uses this fetch path. Any existing comments stored under non-`TASK` conversation types (the codebase tests model task-comment conversations as `TEAM_CHAT`) will now be treated as missing, causing regressions for update/delete/star on existing data.

Delivery Loop review phase blocked. Fix all blocking Deep/Carmack findings, then signal phaseComplete: true again.

Deep review blocking findings:
1. [HIGH] Raw SQL path still excludes only TASK referenceType (logic_regression)
In `apps/dashboard/lib/core/communications/conversations/list-conversations.ts`, `buildWhereClause()` was updated to exclude all `ENTITY_COMMENT_REFERENCE_TYPES`, but `buildBusinessFiltersSql()` still applies `conv.reference_type != 'TASK'` for `excludeTaskComments`. The raw-SQL path is used for read/unread filtering (`fetchOrderedConversationPage` and read-status count queries), so results diverge by code path: PATIENT/ELIGIBILITY_CHECK/CONTACT/PAYER entity-comment conversations are excluded in Prisma mode but still returned in SQL mode.

Carmack review blocking findings:
1. [HIGH] Comment update writes before validating entity reference (atomicity/idempotency)
In `apps/dashboard/lib/core/communications/channels/comments/update-comment.ts`, `updateCommentCommunication` now updates the communication first and only then calls `extractEntityReference`. If the conversation lacks a valid `{referenceType, referenceId}` (legacy/bad data/path misuse), the function throws after the write has already committed. This creates non-deterministic retry behavior (caller sees error but body changed) and violates mutation atomicity.
2. [HIGH] Comment mutation helpers are no longer scoped to comment conversations (architectural-correctness)
Multiple helpers removed conversation scoping (`fetchExistingCommentText`, `fetchCommentForDeletion`, and `toggleStarComment` lookup path now rely only on `id + organizationId` plus loose reference extraction). This weakens the comments bounded-context invariant and allows these APIs to operate on non-comment communications that happen to carry reference metadata, which is a regression in safety and can cause cross-channel data corruption.

Delivery Loop review phase blocked. Fix all blocking Deep/Carmack findings, then signal phaseComplete: true again.

Deep review blocking findings:
1. [HIGH] Comment mutation/read helpers no longer constrain to comment conversation type (authorization/data-integrity)
After the refactor, comment-targeting queries were widened from `conversation.type = TASK` to only `referenceType in ENTITY_COMMENT_REFERENCE_TYPES` in multiple paths (notably `helpers/fetch-comment-text.ts`, `helpers/fetch-for-deletion.ts`, and `channels/comments/toggle-star-comment.ts`). This allows update/delete/star/get-reference flows to operate on any communication attached to an entity reference, including non-comment threads, which is a behavioral regression and can mutate/delete the wrong records.

Carmack review blocking findings:
1. [HIGH] Comment mutations are not scoped to the comment channel and can target unrelated entity-linked conversations (architectural-correctness)
Multiple comment paths now identify a "comment" only by `conversation.referenceType in ENTITY_COMMENT_REFERENCE_TYPES` and `referenceId != null` (for example in `helpers/fetch-for-deletion.ts`, `helpers/fetch-comment-text.ts`, and `toggle-star-comment.ts`). After expanding reference types to PATIENT/ELIGIBILITY_CHECK/CONTACT/PAYER, this can match non-comment conversations that happen to carry those references, allowing update/delete/star behavior to apply to the wrong communication class.
2. [HIGH] Entity authorization/existence validation is outside the write transaction, enabling TOCTOU races (race-safety)
`createCommentCommunication` performs `validateCommentAuthorization` (which calls entity validators) before entering the transaction that creates conversation/communication rows. A concurrent delete/access change can occur between validation and insert, producing orphaned references or accepting writes that should be rejected at commit time.

Delivery Loop review phase blocked. Fix all blocking Deep/Carmack findings, then signal phaseComplete: true again.

Deep review blocking findings:
1. [HIGH] Legacy TEAM_CHAT comment threads are not handled during comment creation resource lookup (behavioral-regression)
In `apps/dashboard/lib/core/communications/channels/comments/helpers/fetch-resources.ts`, both `existingParticipantIds` and `parentCommunication` queries still hardcode `conversation.type: ConversationType.TASK`. The rest of this PR introduces `COMMENT_CONVERSATION_TYPES` specifically to support TASK + legacy TEAM_CHAT comment channels, and other helpers were updated accordingly. This mismatch causes failures/regressions for legacy comment threads: replying with `parentId` on TEAM_CHAT comments resolves `parentCommunication` as null (leading to `Parent comment not found`), and participant carry-forward misses existing TEAM_CHAT participants.

Carmack review blocking findings:
1. [HIGH] Reply path rejects legacy TEAM_CHAT comment threads (regression/race-safety)
In `comments/helpers/fetch-resources.ts`, parent lookup for replies is hard-coded to `conversation.type: ConversationType.TASK` while the codebase explicitly treats comments as both TASK (current) and TEAM_CHAT (legacy). For existing legacy task comment threads, creating a reply with `parentId` will fail `parentCommunication` lookup and then throw `Parent comment not found`. This violates the acceptance requirement that existing task comments continue to work and creates non-deterministic behavior depending on historical conversation type.

Delivery Loop review phase blocked. Fix all blocking Deep/Carmack findings, then signal phaseComplete: true again.

Deep review failed to execute: "Request failed with status code 408"

Delivery Loop review phase blocked. Fix all blocking Deep/Carmack findings, then signal phaseComplete: true again.

Deep review blocking findings:
1. [HIGH] Stale `createCommentCommunication` call still uses removed `taskId` input (type-safety/runtime-regression)
After generalizing comments, `createCommentCommunication` now requires `{ referenceType, referenceId }` instead of `taskId`. `apps/dashboard/lib/core/scheduling/scheduled-visits/reassign-visit.ts` still calls it with `{ taskId, authorId, text }` (around line 288). This is a blocking API mismatch that will fail `pnpm typecheck:native` (and prevents runtime compatibility).

Delivery Loop review phase blocked. Fix all blocking Deep/Carmack findings, then signal phaseComplete: true again.

Deep review blocking findings:
1. [HIGH] Inbox filter now hides non-comment entity conversations (behavioral-regression)
`excludeTaskComments` was changed to exclude any conversation whose `referenceType` is in `ENTITY_COMMENT_REFERENCE_TYPES` (in both SQL and Prisma paths), without constraining by conversation channel/type. `ConversationReferenceType.PATIENT` is used by normal non-comment conversations (see `conversations/create-conversation.ts`), so those conversations will now be incorrectly removed from inbox results when `excludeTaskComments=true`.

Delivery Loop review phase blocked. Fix all blocking Deep/Carmack findings, then signal phaseComplete: true again.

Deep review blocking findings:
1. [HIGH] Mention notifications on comment creation lose author identity (behavioral-regression)
In `apps/dashboard/lib/core/communications/channels/comments/create-comment.ts`, `triggerMentionNotifications` is now called with `{ id: authorId, name: null }` instead of the resolved author record. This regresses existing task-comment behavior by causing author name to be missing (commonly rendered as `Unknown`) in mention notifications for newly created comments.

Carmack review blocking findings:
1. [HIGH] Task mention notifications lost author identity after refactor (behavioral-regression)
In `create-comment.ts`, `triggerMentionNotifications` is now called with `author: { id: authorId, name: null }` instead of the resolved author name. `send-notifications.ts` falls back to `"Unknown"`, so all task mention emails/events from this path lose deterministic actor attribution. This is a regression in existing task-comment behavior and breaks expected identity context for notifications/audit trails.

Delivery Loop review phase blocked. Fix all blocking Deep/Carmack findings, then signal phaseComplete: true again.

Deep review failed to execute: "Request failed with status code 408"

Carmack review blocking findings:
1. [HIGH] toggleStarCommentProcedure swallows intentional TRPCError and returns 500 (error-handling)
In `apps/dashboard/trpc/routers/web/tasks/tasks/procedures/toggle-star-comment.ts`, the new preflight check throws `TRPCError({ code: "BAD_REQUEST" ... })` when the comment is not TASK-linked, but the surrounding `catch` does not rethrow existing `TRPCError`s. It only special-cases a few `Error.message` strings and otherwise throws `INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR`. This converts a deterministic client error into a server error and breaks contract behavior for edge cases.

tasks.title: Define centralized entity-comment reference constants
acceptanceCriteria: `ENTITY_COMMENT_REFERENCE_TYPES` is defined and exported from a stable module
Constant is typed against `ConversationReferenceType` and reused by filtering logic
No new magic-string reference type checks are introduced

tasks.title: Create entity validator registry for comment authorization/existence
acceptanceCriteria: Registry file exists and is used by comment validation flow
TASK, PATIENT, and ELIGIBILITY_CHECK validators verify entity existence and org access
CONTACT and PAYER entries exist as explicit stubs (no implicit fallthrough)

tasks.title: Refactor core comment helpers to generic entity references
acceptanceCriteria: Task-specific helper names/signatures in the ticket are replaced by generalized versions
Helper internals no longer hardcode `ConversationReferenceType.TASK`
Parent comment validation still enforces same-reference threading safety

tasks.title: Extend Prisma schema for generalized references
acceptanceCriteria: ConversationReferenceType enum contains TASK, PATIENT, ELIGIBILITY_CHECK, CONTACT, PAYER
EligibilityCheck model has nullable `manualVerificationNotes` field
Prisma migration is created/applied and `pnpm --filter database generate` succeeds

tasks.title: Add/adjust tests and run required quality gates
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover TASK, PATIENT, ELIGIBILITY_CHECK validator and listing/create paths
Tests verify inbox exclusion for all values in `ENTITY_COMMENT_REFERENCE_TYPES` and inclusion for null referenceType
All checks pass: `pnpm lint:fix && pnpm format:fix && pnpm typecheck:native && pnpm lint:custom`

tasks.title: Update comment channel APIs to use referenceType + referenceId
acceptanceCriteria: `createCommentCommunication` accepts `{ referenceType, referenceId }`
`listCommentCommunications` accepts `{ referenceType, referenceId }`
Existing task comment flows still function without behavioral regression

tasks.title: Align update/delete/star comment flows with generalized reference extraction
acceptanceCriteria: No remaining internal dependency on `extractTaskReference`
Task router DTOs still receive task IDs for task comments
Non-task comment references no longer fail due to TASK-only assumptions in shared channel code

tasks.title: Replace TASK-only inbox exclusion with entity-comment exclusion set
acceptanceCriteria: Filter no longer uses `{ not: ConversationReferenceType.TASK }`
Filter uses `{ not: { in: ENTITY_COMMENT_REFERENCE_TYPES } }` with null-safe OR behavior
`excludeTaskComments` test suite is updated to cover multi-entity exclusion

blockingFindings.title: Task delete route can delete non-task comments before rejecting
blockingFindings.detail: In `apps/dashboard/trpc/routers/web/tasks/tasks/procedures/delete-task-comment.ts`, task association is checked only after `deleteCommentCommunication()` runs. Since `fetchCommentForDeletion()` no longer constrains conversation reference type to TASK, a caller can delete a PATIENT/ELIGIBILITY_CHECK/etc comment by ID, then receive `BAD_REQUEST` after the deletion already committed.
blockingFindings.suggestedFix: Enforce expected reference type before mutation. Either: (1) in the task procedure, fetch and validate `conversation.referenceType === TASK` before calling delete; or (2) add an `expectedReferenceType` parameter to `deleteCommentCommunication` and include it in the deletion precondition query so non-TASK comments are never deleted through task endpoints.

blockingFindings.title: Task update/star routes can mutate non-task comments before rejecting
blockingFindings.detail: In `update-task-comment.ts` and `toggle-star-comment.ts`, task association is derived in `buildCommentDto` after `updateCommentCommunication()` / `toggleStarComment()` have already written changes. Core helpers now accept any entity reference and no longer enforce TASK-only behavior, so task-scoped endpoints can update/star comments for other entities and then throw `BAD_REQUEST` after the write.
blockingFindings.suggestedFix: Validate `referenceType === TASK` before performing write operations, or enforce it inside core mutation queries via an `expectedReferenceType` guard. The guard must be part of the DB read/write precondition to prevent side effects on non-task comments.

blockingFindings.title: Entity validators do not enforce per-user access
blockingFindings.detail: The new validator contract in `entity-validators.ts` only accepts `(organizationId, referenceId)` and `validateCommentAuthorization` only verifies org membership before calling it. This cannot satisfy the requirement that validators ensure the user has access to the referenced entity; it only checks entity existence in-org. For non-task entities, this can allow comment creation on records a user should not access.
blockingFindings.suggestedFix: Change `EntityValidator` to include `authorId` (or full auth context) and implement entity-specific access checks (not just existence) for TASK, PATIENT, and ELIGIBILITY_CHECK. Update `validateCommentAuthorization` and all call sites accordingly.

blockingFindings.title: Recipient discovery is no longer scoped to comment-thread conversations
blockingFindings.detail: In `helpers/fetch-resources.ts`, `existingParticipantIds` now queries by `(referenceType, referenceId)` only, after removing conversation-type scoping. If any non-comment conversation shares that entity reference, its participants are pulled into comment recipient construction, causing unintended participant propagation and potential PHI exposure.
blockingFindings.suggestedFix: Reintroduce a strict discriminator for comment conversations in participant lookup (e.g., conversation type/channel marker dedicated to entity comments, or equivalent invariant), and apply the same discriminator anywhere comment-thread resources are fetched.

blockingFindings.title: Comment listing query no longer scopes to comment threads
blockingFindings.detail: In `apps/dashboard/lib/core/communications/channels/comments/list-comments.ts`, the `findMany` filter now matches only `conversation.referenceType` + `referenceId`. The previous task-scoped guard (`conversation.type === TASK`) was removed, and there is still no `communication.kind === COMMENT` guard. This can pull in non-comment communications from other conversation types that reuse the same reference pair, causing timeline pollution and potential cross-channel data exposure.
blockingFindings.suggestedFix: Restore strict scoping in `listCommentCommunications` by filtering to comment-thread conversations and comment records, e.g. add `conversation: { type: ConversationType.TASK, referenceType, referenceId }` and `kind: CommunicationKind.COMMENT` (or the project’s equivalent canonical comment discriminator).

blockingFindings.title: Comment listing query no longer constrains conversation channel type
blockingFindings.detail: In apps/dashboard/lib/core/communications/channels/comments/list-comments.ts:36-43, the query now filters only by {referenceType, referenceId} and dropped the prior ConversationType guard. This allows any future/current non-comment conversation that reuses the same entity reference to be returned as a comment, which breaks channel isolation and can surface incorrect timeline data.
blockingFindings.suggestedFix: Reintroduce explicit channel scoping in the where clause (e.g. conversation: { type: ConversationType.TASK, referenceType, referenceId }) and add a regression test proving non-comment conversations with the same reference are excluded.

blockingFindings.title: Deletion/reference lookup now hard-requires `ConversationType.TASK`, breaking legacy entity-comment conversations
blockingFindings.detail: `fetchCommentForDeletion` was changed from filtering by `conversation.referenceType === TASK` to `conversation.type === TASK` (apps/dashboard/lib/core/communications/channels/comments/helpers/fetch-for-deletion.ts). The new task TRPC flows (`delete-task-comment`, `toggle-star-comment`, `update-task-comment`) now depend on `getCommentEntityReference`, which uses this fetch path. Any existing comments stored under non-`TASK` conversation types (the codebase tests model task-comment conversations as `TEAM_CHAT`) will now be treated as missing, causing regressions for update/delete/star on existing data.
blockingFindings.suggestedFix: Do not gate entity-comment lookup by `conversation.type === TASK`. Filter by presence/validity of `referenceType`/`referenceId` (or by allowed entity-comment reference types), and keep compatibility with historical conversation types. Add regression tests covering legacy conversation type data.

blockingFindings.title: Raw SQL path still excludes only TASK referenceType
blockingFindings.detail: In `apps/dashboard/lib/core/communications/conversations/list-conversations.ts`, `buildWhereClause()` was updated to exclude all `ENTITY_COMMENT_REFERENCE_TYPES`, but `buildBusinessFiltersSql()` still applies `conv.reference_type != 'TASK'` for `excludeTaskComments`. The raw-SQL path is used for read/unread filtering (`fetchOrderedConversationPage` and read-status count queries), so results diverge by code path: PATIENT/ELIGIBILITY_CHECK/CONTACT/PAYER entity-comment conversations are excluded in Prisma mode but still returned in SQL mode.
blockingFindings.suggestedFix: Update `buildBusinessFiltersSql()` to mirror `buildWhereClause()` by excluding the full `ENTITY_COMMENT_REFERENCE_TYPES` set (and keep NULL-inclusive behavior). Also reuse that same SQL condition in the read-status count query path so pagination and totals stay consistent.

blockingFindings.title: Comment update writes before validating entity reference
blockingFindings.detail: In `apps/dashboard/lib/core/communications/channels/comments/update-comment.ts`, `updateCommentCommunication` now updates the communication first and only then calls `extractEntityReference`. If the conversation lacks a valid `{referenceType, referenceId}` (legacy/bad data/path misuse), the function throws after the write has already committed. This creates non-deterministic retry behavior (caller sees error but body changed) and violates mutation atomicity.
blockingFindings.suggestedFix: Validate entity reference before any write. Either (a) prefetch communication+conversation and call `extractEntityReference` first, then update, or (b) constrain the update query itself with conversation predicates that guarantee a valid entity-comment reference and return NOT_FOUND if not matched.

blockingFindings.title: Comment mutation helpers are no longer scoped to comment conversations
blockingFindings.detail: Multiple helpers removed conversation scoping (`fetchExistingCommentText`, `fetchCommentForDeletion`, and `toggleStarComment` lookup path now rely only on `id + organizationId` plus loose reference extraction). This weakens the comments bounded-context invariant and allows these APIs to operate on non-comment communications that happen to carry reference metadata, which is a regression in safety and can cause cross-channel data corruption.
blockingFindings.suggestedFix: Reintroduce strict comment-channel predicates in all comment read/mutation entry points (at minimum conversation type and allowed entity reference types) and keep that invariant enforced in core helpers, not only in specific tRPC procedures.

blockingFindings.title: Comment mutation/read helpers no longer constrain to comment conversation type
blockingFindings.detail: After the refactor, comment-targeting queries were widened from `conversation.type = TASK` to only `referenceType in ENTITY_COMMENT_REFERENCE_TYPES` in multiple paths (notably `helpers/fetch-comment-text.ts`, `helpers/fetch-for-deletion.ts`, and `channels/comments/toggle-star-comment.ts`). This allows update/delete/star/get-reference flows to operate on any communication attached to an entity reference, including non-comment threads, which is a behavioral regression and can mutate/delete the wrong records.
blockingFindings.suggestedFix: Reintroduce a strict comment-thread discriminator in all comment mutation/read queries (at minimum add `conversation.type: ConversationType.TASK` where applicable, or use a dedicated comment type flag if available) so comment APIs only ever target comment communications.

blockingFindings.title: Comment mutations are not scoped to the comment channel and can target unrelated entity-linked conversations
blockingFindings.detail: Multiple comment paths now identify a "comment" only by `conversation.referenceType in ENTITY_COMMENT_REFERENCE_TYPES` and `referenceId != null` (for example in `helpers/fetch-for-deletion.ts`, `helpers/fetch-comment-text.ts`, and `toggle-star-comment.ts`). After expanding reference types to PATIENT/ELIGIBILITY_CHECK/CONTACT/PAYER, this can match non-comment conversations that happen to carry those references, allowing update/delete/star behavior to apply to the wrong communication class.
blockingFindings.suggestedFix: Add an explicit channel discriminator to all comment selectors (at minimum `conversation.type = ConversationType.TASK`, or preferably a dedicated comment conversation type/channel marker) and apply it consistently in fetch/update/delete/star/reference helper queries.

blockingFindings.title: Entity authorization/existence validation is outside the write transaction, enabling TOCTOU races
blockingFindings.detail: `createCommentCommunication` performs `validateCommentAuthorization` (which calls entity validators) before entering the transaction that creates conversation/communication rows. A concurrent delete/access change can occur between validation and insert, producing orphaned references or accepting writes that should be rejected at commit time.
blockingFindings.suggestedFix: Re-run entity validation inside the same transaction used for writes (or move all validation into that transaction) and use row-level locking/transactional checks where possible to ensure validation and insert are atomic.

blockingFindings.title: Legacy TEAM_CHAT comment threads are not handled during comment creation resource lookup
blockingFindings.detail: In `apps/dashboard/lib/core/communications/channels/comments/helpers/fetch-resources.ts`, both `existingParticipantIds` and `parentCommunication` queries still hardcode `conversation.type: ConversationType.TASK`. The rest of this PR introduces `COMMENT_CONVERSATION_TYPES` specifically to support TASK + legacy TEAM_CHAT comment channels, and other helpers were updated accordingly. This mismatch causes failures/regressions for legacy comment threads: replying with `parentId` on TEAM_CHAT comments resolves `parentCommunication` as null (leading to `Parent comment not found`), and participant carry-forward misses existing TEAM_CHAT participants.
blockingFindings.suggestedFix: Update `fetchCommentResources` conversation filters to use `type: { in: [...COMMENT_CONVERSATION_TYPES] }` (or equivalent shared predicate) for both participant and parent lookups, matching the other comment helpers in this PR.

blockingFindings.title: Reply path rejects legacy TEAM_CHAT comment threads
blockingFindings.detail: In `comments/helpers/fetch-resources.ts`, parent lookup for replies is hard-coded to `conversation.type: ConversationType.TASK` while the codebase explicitly treats comments as both TASK (current) and TEAM_CHAT (legacy). For existing legacy task comment threads, creating a reply with `parentId` will fail `parentCommunication` lookup and then throw `Parent comment not found`. This violates the acceptance requirement that existing task comments continue to work and creates non-deterministic behavior depending on historical conversation type.
blockingFindings.suggestedFix: Use the shared comment conversation type set (`COMMENT_CONVERSATION_TYPES`) for parent lookup (and participant discovery in the same helper) so both TASK and TEAM_CHAT comment threads are handled consistently.

blockingFindings.title: Stale `createCommentCommunication` call still uses removed `taskId` input
blockingFindings.detail: After generalizing comments, `createCommentCommunication` now requires `{ referenceType, referenceId }` instead of `taskId`. `apps/dashboard/lib/core/scheduling/scheduled-visits/reassign-visit.ts` still calls it with `{ taskId, authorId, text }` (around line 288). This is a blocking API mismatch that will fail `pnpm typecheck:native` (and prevents runtime compatibility).
blockingFindings.suggestedFix: Update that call site to pass `referenceType: ConversationReferenceType.TASK` and `referenceId: taskId`, and import `ConversationReferenceType` from `@/database/enums` in `reassign-visit.ts`.

blockingFindings.title: Inbox filter now hides non-comment entity conversations
blockingFindings.detail: `excludeTaskComments` was changed to exclude any conversation whose `referenceType` is in `ENTITY_COMMENT_REFERENCE_TYPES` (in both SQL and Prisma paths), without constraining by conversation channel/type. `ConversationReferenceType.PATIENT` is used by normal non-comment conversations (see `conversations/create-conversation.ts`), so those conversations will now be incorrectly removed from inbox results when `excludeTaskComments=true`.
blockingFindings.suggestedFix: Scope exclusion to actual comment threads by combining reference-type exclusion with comment conversation type checks (e.g., `type IN COMMENT_CONVERSATION_TYPES`) in both `buildBusinessFiltersSql` and `buildWhereClause` in `list-conversations.ts`. Update tests to verify patient-linked non-comment conversations are still included.

blockingFindings.title: Mention notifications on comment creation lose author identity
blockingFindings.detail: In `apps/dashboard/lib/core/communications/channels/comments/create-comment.ts`, `triggerMentionNotifications` is now called with `{ id: authorId, name: null }` instead of the resolved author record. This regresses existing task-comment behavior by causing author name to be missing (commonly rendered as `Unknown`) in mention notifications for newly created comments.
blockingFindings.suggestedFix: Preserve and pass the resolved author object (or fetch sender user by `authorId`) when calling `triggerMentionNotifications`, so notification payloads include the correct author name as before.

blockingFindings.title: Task mention notifications lost author identity after refactor
blockingFindings.detail: In `create-comment.ts`, `triggerMentionNotifications` is now called with `author: { id: authorId, name: null }` instead of the resolved author name. `send-notifications.ts` falls back to `"Unknown"`, so all task mention emails/events from this path lose deterministic actor attribution. This is a regression in existing task-comment behavior and breaks expected identity context for notifications/audit trails.
blockingFindings.suggestedFix: Carry the fetched author name through the transaction result (or re-fetch author once) and pass the real `{ id, name }` into `triggerMentionNotifications` in `createCommentCommunication`.

blockingFindings.title: toggleStarCommentProcedure swallows intentional TRPCError and returns 500
blockingFindings.detail: In `apps/dashboard/trpc/routers/web/tasks/tasks/procedures/toggle-star-comment.ts`, the new preflight check throws `TRPCError({ code: "BAD_REQUEST" ... })` when the comment is not TASK-linked, but the surrounding `catch` does not rethrow existing `TRPCError`s. It only special-cases a few `Error.message` strings and otherwise throws `INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR`. This converts a deterministic client error into a server error and breaks contract behavior for edge cases.
blockingFindings.suggestedFix: At the top of the `catch`, add `if (error instanceof TRPCError) throw error;` (as done in `delete-task-comment.ts`) before message-based mapping.

deep.title: Mention notifications on comment creation lose author identity
Raw SQL path still excludes only TASK referenceType
Comment mutation/read helpers no longer constrain to comment conversation type
Inbox filter now hides non-comment entity conversations
Legacy TEAM_CHAT comment threads are not handled during comment creation resource lookup
Task delete route can delete non-task comments before rejecting
Task update/star routes can mutate non-task comments before rejecting
Comment listing query no longer scopes to comment threads
Stale `createCommentCommunication` call still uses removed `taskId` input

carmack.title: toggleStarCommentProcedure swallows intentional TRPCError and returns 500
Task mention notifications lost author identity after refactor
Comment update writes before validating entity reference
Comment mutation helpers are no longer scoped to comment conversations
Comment mutations are not scoped to the comment channel and can target unrelated entity-linked conversations
Entity authorization/existence validation is outside the write transaction, enabling TOCTOU races
Deletion/reference lookup now hard-requires `ConversationType.TASK`, breaking legacy entity-comment conversations
Reply path rejects legacy TEAM_CHAT comment threads
Entity validators do not enforce per-user access
Recipient discovery is no longer scoped to comment-thread conversations
Comment listing query no longer constrains conversation channel type

payloadSummary.planText: Implement Phase 1 of generalized entity comments by extending schema enums/fields, introducing an entity validator registry, refactoring comment helpers and channel APIs from task-only to referenceType+referenceId, updating inbox exclusion logic, and adding regression coverage to guarantee existing task comment behavior remains intact.

payloadSummary.summary: Deep and Carmack review gates passed.
No UI-facing files changed; browser smoke gate skipped.
